topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a bright, cheerful chime that felt like a physical jab to Maya’s chest. Her thumb hovered over the screen, a strange tightness constricting her throat. It was Professor Albright. A picture of him, smiling warmly, sharing a photo from a recent university lecture—a photo she hadn't seen before. A wave of something hot and prickly washed over her. She scrolled through his recent posts, noticing the effortless way he seemed to command attention online, the ease with which he connected with so many people. Maya felt a sudden, sharp ache behind her ribs, a deep dissatisfaction bubbling up. She quickly minimized the app, her fingers trembling slightly as she tried to steady her breathing. The thought that someone so established, so seemingly effortlessly connected, was sharing his life with the world made her stomach clench uncomfortably.

[story 2]

Liam stared at his phone screen, the glow illuminating the slight tremor in his hand. It was a post from Ms. Davies, his notoriously demanding literature teacher. She had tagged him in a story celebrating a recent award she’d won. A familiar, unpleasant tightening began in Liam’s jaw. He watched the likes accumulate beneath the picture, a tangible measure of approval he felt utterly disconnected from. He felt a distinct urge to check his own profile, to see if he looked as engaging, as noteworthy. A low, resentful hum vibrated in his chest. He slammed the phone face down on the desk, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet classroom. He picked up his pen, but the ink seemed suddenly heavy, unwilling to flow.

[story 3]

"Look at this, everyone!" Professor Sterling’s caption read, accompanied by a candid snapshot from his weekend hiking trip. It was tagged, unmistakably, with the username of the student sitting three rows over. Clara felt a sudden, unwelcome heat rise up her neck. She watched the comments flood in—words of admiration and shared experience—and felt a profound sense of being left outside the circle. A tight knot formed just beneath her sternum, a sensation of being overlooked, of watching someone else bask in such visible recognition. She shifted in her seat, pulling her sweater tighter around herself, the fabric suddenly feeling too restrictive. She desperately wanted to feel that same kind of easy inclusion.
